classOfService.note: Duration is per sector (one leg of the journey), not the whole trip. All domestic travel is Economy Class regardless of level.

rows.level: C-Suite, Directors, Country Heads, Global Leaders

classOfService.durationNote: Short-haul = 6 hrs or less · Long-haul = more than 6 hrs

perDiemByZone.note: Covers meals, snacks and beverages only. Paid in advance — no receipts required. Does not cover personal expenses.

rows.countries: United States, Canada, Europe, Australia, East Asia & Singapore
South America, Middle East & Southeast Asia (excl. Singapore)
South Asia, Africa and any other countries

hotelLimitsByZone.note: For short-term international travel (up to 3 weeks). Bed & Breakfast basis. Exceeding these limits needs prior approval.

keyRules: Submit Travel Request Form (TRF) approval at least 1 month before travel where possible, to allow for visa processing and the best rates.
Emergency travel (departing within 48 hrs): retroactive TRF is allowed, but you still need prior written/email approval from your supervisor. Submit the TRF within 3 days of completing travel.
Use the same airline, direct flights, and round-trip tickets whenever feasible.
Class upgrades for medical reasons require a doctor's note.
Hotel booking must be on Bed & Breakfast basis, close to the workplace or visit location. Mini bar and in-room alcohol are not reimbursable.
Post Travel Expense Report is due within 10 business days of returning, with original itemized receipts attached.
Reimbursement is processed within 14 business days of a complete, approved claim.
Business trips exceeding 30 consecutive days are treated as temporary assignments and need prior approval.
If combining business travel with personal time, only the business-related portion is reimbursable.

approvalMatrix.note: Applies to international business travel. All domestic travel is approved by the relevant Country Head.

rows.firstApproval: Dept. Head, Country Head, Regional Head
